title: Ensure Second Salesforce Instance Does Not Block Primary Instance Usage
story: As a DataTools Pro user with multiple Salesforce orgs, I want to add a second Salesforce instance without losing access to my primary instance, so that I can use Dictionary, ERD, and Metrics across both orgs.
description:
When a user signs up with a Sandbox and adds a second production or sandbox instance, the expectation is that both instances remain usable for all DataTools features (Dictionary, ERD, Metrics).

### Architecture
- Second instance uses completely separate tables (user info, instance info, objects, metadata) that are independent from the primary instance
- Users can log out and re-authenticate with either instance

### Limitation
A customer cannot add the same instance used for initial authentication as a second instance. Any other production or sandbox instance can be added.

### UX Enhancement
When adding a second instance, add explanatory text clarifying this is for data migration mapping.
acceptance_criteria: Second Salesforce instance can be added without blocking primary instance
Both instances are independently accessible after logout/login
Second instance data is stored in separate tables from primary
Explanatory text shown when adding second instance
ux_changes: Explanatory text when adding second instance clarifying migration mapping purpose.
middleware_changes: Second instance uses independent data tables. Auth flow supports switching between instances.
data_model_changes: Separate tables for second instance: user information, instance information, objects, and metadata.
implementation_notes: Waqar verified the existing architecture already supports multi-instance. Second instance uses separate tables. Only limitation: can't add the same instance as both primary and secondary.
comment_summary: Waqar reviewed the code and tested the workflow end-to-end with a Trailhead Playground. Confirmed architecture supports multiple instances with separate data tables. Requested a customer meeting to investigate the specific user's issue.
